title: OK Senate HHS Committee Votes in Favor of Murdering 10,000 Babies
author: James Silberman
orientation: Silberman responds to the Oklahoma Senate Health and Human Services Committee killing SB495, naming the ten senators who voted to leave abortion legal and framing the loss as a tragedy that nevertheless steels the abolitionist movement to keep working.

questions.question: Which Oklahoma senators are named as voting to keep abortion legal in the HHS committee?
questions.answer: Ten senators are named: Greg McCortney, Paul Rosino, John Haste, Jessica Garvin, Julie Daniels, Rob Standridge, Frank Simpson, George Young, Carri Hicks, and Jo Anna Dossett, with the last three identified as Democrats and the rest as pro-life Republicans.
questions.quote: Greg McCortney, Paul Rosino, John Haste, Jessica Garvin, Julie Daniels, Rob Standridge, Frank Simpson, George Young, Carri Hicks, and Jo Anna Dossett voted for the murder of 10,000 babies in Oklahoma over the next two years. All but the last three of that list are pro-life Republicans.

questions.question: Does the death of SB495 mean abortion is guaranteed to remain legal in Oklahoma until 2023?
questions.answer: No. Silberman insists the fight is not finished and that other paths remain, even though SB495 was the strongest vehicle currently on the table.
questions.quote: It is not a guarantee that abortion will remain legal until 2023. There are still a few options at our disposal now that SB495 is dead. But this bill was the best chance to rescue those being led to slaughter.

questions.question: How does the article frame the emotional and moral weight of the committee's vote?
questions.answer: It refuses to soften the loss, calling it a tragedy worth weeping over and a betrayal worth righteous anger, while insisting that faithfulness rather than measurable success is what God demands.
questions.quote: There's no pretending this isn't a tragedy. It makes us weep for the lives of the children who are likely to be murdered. It makes us righteously angry with the spineless individuals who have no regard for God or humanity. But we also know that all we can do is be faithful, and we will never relent or give up this fight.

questions.question: What historical figure does Silberman invoke to model perseverance in the face of legislative failure?
questions.answer: He cites John Quincy Adams, who responded to questions about congressional inaction on slavery by declaring duty was his and results belonged to God.
questions.quote: When a reporter asked John Quincy Adams if he was discouraged by the lack of progress within Congress regarding the abolition of slavery, he responded, "Duty is ours. Results are God's."

questions.question: What does Free the States plan to do in the wake of SB495's defeat?
questions.answer: Continue the same educational work directed at both the public and elected officials in Oklahoma, with the goal of abolishing abortion entirely.
questions.quote: We will continue educating the people of Oklahoma and the public officials of Oklahoma of their duty to rescue those being led to slaughter. That's all we've ever done. That's what we're going to do until the day abortion is abolished.

questions.question: Why does the author argue that pro-life politicians killing abolition bills actually grows the movement?
questions.answer: Because exposure to that hypocrisy is what radicalizes many activists. Silberman says watching pro-life officials and lobbyists oppose abolition has been a recruiting event for the movement.
questions.quote: Our resolve is only strengthened and our movement only grows every time pro-life politicians show their true colors. Many of the most active members of the Abolitionist Movement came into the movement when they saw pro-life politicians and lobbyists oppose bills to abolish abortion.

questions.question: What 19th-century abolitionist quotation does the article draw on for hope after defeat?
questions.answer: It quotes William Lloyd Garrison's claim that personal defeats do not erase the eventual triumph of truth, justice, reason, and humanity.
questions.quote: As William Lloyd Garrison said, "We may be personally defeated, but our principles never. Truth, Justice, Reason, Humanity, must and will gloriously triumph. Already a host is coming up to the help of the Lord against the mighty, and the prospect before us is full of encouragement."

questions.question: How is the article equipping readers to take direct action after the vote?
questions.answer: By publishing the official email addresses of every senator who voted against SB495 so constituents can contact them directly.
questions.quote: Email addresses of the Senators who voted today for the murder of 10,000 children: greg.mccortney@oksenate.gov, paul.rosino@oksenate.gov, julie.daniels@oksenate.gov, john.haste@oksenate.gov, carri.hicks@oksenate.gov, george.young@oksenate.gov, frank.simpson@oksenate.gov, rob.standridge@oksenate.gov, joanna.dossett@oksenate.gov, jessica.garvin@oksenate.gov

questions.question: What outreach tools does Free the States recommend for converting pro-lifers and pro-choicers?
questions.answer: The 'Are You An Abolitionist' pamphlet for converting pro-lifers and the 'Abortion is Murder And Everybody Knows it' quadfold for engaging pro-aborts, both available through the Free the States store.
questions.quote: The best brief resource in the Abolitionist Movement for turning pro-lifers into abolitionists is the [Are You An Abolitionist pamphlet](https://store.abolitionistsrising.com/products/are-you-an-abolitionist). The best quadfold for outreach to pro-aborts and evangelism is the [Abortion is Murder And Everybody Knows it quadfold](https://store.abolitionistsrising.com/collections/dropcards-quadfolds).
